prod-001.name: Pan-Seared Cod with Lemon Butter
ingredients: 2 fresh cod loin portions (approx. 150 g each)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Pat the cod loins dry with kitchen paper and season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Place cod loins presentation-side down. Cook without moving for 3–4 minutes until a golden crust forms.
Flip carefully. Add butter, garlic, and lemon zest to the pan.
Tilt the pan and baste the cod with the foaming butter for 2–3 minutes until the fish is opaque throughout.
Remove from heat. Squeeze lemon juice over the fish and scatter with fresh parsley.
Serve immediately with steamed new potatoes or a green salad.

prod-001.name: Plokkfiskur — Traditional Icelandic Mashed Fish
ingredients: 400 g fresh cod loin, skin removed
500 g floury potatoes, peeled and quartered
Salt and white pepper to taste
Rye bread and butter, to serve
steps: Place the cod in a saucepan, cover with cold water, and bring to a gentle simmer. Poach for 8–10 minutes until the fish flakes easily. Remove and flake into large pieces, discarding any bones.
Meanwhile, boil the potatoes in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash roughly — some lumps are traditional.
In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Sauté the onion for 5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the mashed potato and flaked fish to the onion. Stir gently to combine.
Pour in the milk gradually, stirring over low heat until you reach a creamy but chunky consistency.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Serve hot in bowls with dark Icelandic rye bread and cold butter.

prod-001.name: Cod en Papillote with Herbs
ingredients: 2 fresh cod loin portions (approx. 150 g each)
2 tbsp dry white wine or fish stock
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (180 °C fan / gas 6).
Cut two large rectangles of baking parchment (about 40 × 30 cm each).
Divide the courgette slices and cherry tomatoes between the two sheets, placing them in the centre.
Lay a cod loin on top of each vegetable bed. Season with salt and pepper.
Top each portion with a lemon slice, a sprig of thyme, and a sprig of dill.
Drizzle each with 1 tbsp wine and ½ tbsp olive oil.
Fold the parchment over and crimp the edges tightly to seal into parcels.
Place on a baking tray and bake for 14–16 minutes until the parcels are puffed.
Transfer parcels to plates. Let guests open at the table to enjoy the aromatic steam.

prod-002.name: Oven-Baked Cod from Frozen with Herb Crust
ingredients: 2 frozen cod loin portions (approx. 150 g each)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Preheat the oven to 220 °C (200 °C fan / gas 7). Line a baking tray with parchment.
Place the frozen cod loins on the tray. Brush the top of each with Dijon mustard.
In a small bowl, mix panko, parsley, oregano, garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Press the breadcrumb mixture firmly onto the mustard-coated top of each portion.
Bake for 25–28 minutes until the crust is golden and the fish is opaque and flakes easily.
Rest for 1 minute, then serve with lemon wedges and steamed vegetables.

prod-002.name: Plokkfiskur — Traditional Icelandic Mashed Fish
ingredients: 500 g floury potatoes, peeled and quartered
Salt and white pepper to taste
Rye bread and butter, to serve
steps: Place the frozen cod in a saucepan, cover with cold water, and bring to a gentle simmer. Poach for 12–14 minutes until the fish flakes easily. Remove and flake into large pieces, discarding any bones.
Meanwhile, boil the potatoes in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash roughly — some lumps are traditional.
In a large saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Sauté the onion for 5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the mashed potato and flaked fish to the onion. Stir gently to combine.
Pour in the milk gradually, stirring over low heat until you reach a creamy but chunky consistency.
Season with salt and white pepper.
Serve hot in bowls with dark Icelandic rye bread and cold butter.

prod-002.name: Cod and Potato Chowder
ingredients: 2 medium potatoes, peeled and diced (1.5 cm cubes)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Melt butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add onion, celery, and carrot. Cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the diced potatoes, stock, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer for 12 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Place the frozen cod directly into the simmering chowder. Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove the cod, flake into large chunks, and return to the pot.
Stir in the milk and cream. Heat gently — do not boil.
Season with salt and pepper. Remove the bay leaf.
Ladle into warm bowls and garnish with fresh chives. Serve with crusty bread.

prod-003.name: Grilled Haddock with Mustard and Dill
ingredients: 2 fresh haddock loin portions (approx. 150 g each)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Preheat the grill to high. Line a grill tray with foil and lightly oil.
In a small bowl, mix mustard, crème fraîche, half the dill, and lemon juice.
Place haddock loins on the tray. Brush with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill for 4 minutes on the first side.
Flip carefully, then spread the mustard-dill mixture evenly over the top of each piece.
Grill for another 4–5 minutes until the topping is lightly golden and the fish is cooked through.
Scatter with remaining fresh dill. Serve with boiled new potatoes and a cucumber salad.

prod-003.name: Haddock and Spinach en Papillote
ingredients: 2 fresh haddock loin portions (approx. 150 g each)
1 tbsp butter, cut into small pieces
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (180 °C fan / gas 6).
Cut two large sheets of baking parchment (about 40 × 30 cm each).
Divide the baby spinach between the sheets, forming a bed in the centre of each.
Scatter shallot slices and capers over the spinach.
Place a haddock loin on each spinach bed. Season with salt and pepper.
Top each with a lemon slice and dots of butter. Drizzle with 1 tbsp wine each.
Fold the parchment over and crimp the edges tightly to seal.
Bake for 14–16 minutes until the parcels puff up.
Serve in the parcels — open at the table for the full aroma.

prod-003.name: Quick Haddock Ceviche
ingredients: 200 g very fresh haddock loin, skin removed
1 small red onion, very thinly sliced
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely diced
Small handful fresh coriander, roughly chopped
steps: Using a very sharp knife, slice the haddock into thin pieces (about 3 mm thick). Arrange on a chilled plate.
Pour the lime juice evenly over the fish. Allow to sit for 2–3 minutes — the acid will lightly 'cook' the surface.
Scatter the red onion, chilli, avocado, and cherry tomatoes over the fish.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with flaky sea salt.
Finish with fresh coriander. Serve immediately with tortilla chips or toasted sourdough.
Note: Only use sashimi-grade fresh fish for ceviche. The fish is essentially raw in the centre.

prod-004.name: Oven-Baked Haddock from Frozen with Parmesan Crust
ingredients: 2 frozen haddock loin portions (approx. 150 g each)
steps: Preheat the oven to 220 °C (200 °C fan / gas 7). Line a baking tray with parchment.
Place the frozen haddock loins on the tray. Spread a thin layer of mayonnaise on top of each — this helps the crust stick.
In a small bowl, mix panko, Parmesan, parsley, paprika, and olive oil.
Press the Parmesan crumb mixture firmly onto each haddock portion.
Bake for 25–28 minutes until the crust is golden and crisp and the fish is opaque throughout.
Serve with lemon wedges, chips, and mushy peas for a posh fish-and-chips night.

prod-004.name: Haddock and Leek Gratin
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (180 °C fan / gas 6).
Place the frozen haddock in a saucepan, cover with water, and simmer for 12 minutes. Drain, flake into large chunks, and set aside.
In the same pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add the leeks and cook gently for 8 minutes until very soft.
Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually add the milk, stirring constantly, until you have a smooth sauce.
Remove from heat. Stir in half the cheddar and the mustard. Season with salt and white pepper.
Gently fold in the flaked haddock. Pour into a buttered ovenproof dish.
Top with the remaining cheddar and panko breadcrumbs.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until bubbling and golden on top. Serve with crusty bread or mashed potato.

prod-004.name: Haddock Fishcakes
ingredients: 400 g floury potatoes, peeled and quartered
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Boil the potatoes in salted water until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain well and mash until smooth. Allow to cool slightly.
Meanwhile, poach the frozen haddock in simmering water for 12 minutes. Drain, flake finely, and pat dry.
Combine the mashed potato, flaked haddock, spring onions, parsley, lemon zest, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Mix well.
Shape into 8 even patties, about 2 cm thick. Place on a tray and chill in the fridge for 15 minutes to firm up.
Set up a breading station: flour in one shallow dish, beaten egg in another, panko in a third.
Coat each fishcake in flour, then egg, then panko, pressing gently to adhere.
Heat 1 cm of vegetable oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Fry fishcakes in batches for 3–4 minutes per side until golden and crisp.
Drain on kitchen paper. Serve with tartare sauce, lemon wedges, and a simple salad.

prod-005.name: Honey-Soy Glazed Salmon
ingredients: 2 fresh salmon fillet portions (approx. 150 g each), skin on
steps: Whisk together soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and rice vinegar in a small bowl.
Heat a non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat. Place salmon skin-side down and cook for 4 minutes until the skin is crisp.
Flip the salmon. Pour the glaze into the pan around the fish.
Cook for 3–4 minutes, spooning the bubbling glaze over the salmon repeatedly as it thickens.
Remove from heat when the salmon is just cooked through and the glaze is sticky and caramelised.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and sliced spring onion.
Serve immediately with steamed jasmine rice and tenderstem broccoli.

prod-005.name: Grilled Salmon with Avocado Salsa
ingredients: 2 fresh salmon fillet portions (approx. 150 g each), skin on
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
½ small red onion, finely diced
steps: Prepare the salsa: combine avocado, cherry tomatoes, red onion, lime juice, coriander, chilli flakes, and a pinch of salt. Set aside.
Preheat the grill to high. Line a grill tray with foil.
Brush the salmon with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill skin-side down for 5 minutes. Flip and grill for another 3–4 minutes until just cooked through — the centre should still be slightly translucent.
Transfer to plates and spoon the avocado salsa generously over the top.
Serve with a lime wedge and warm flatbread or a leafy salad.

prod-005.name: Salmon and Cream Cheese Bagel
ingredients: 150 g fresh salmon fillet, skin removed
¼ red onion, very thinly sliced
steps: Using a very sharp knife, slice the raw salmon as thinly as possible at a slight angle (sashimi-style).
Spread cream cheese generously on each toasted bagel half.
Arrange the salmon slices over the cream cheese.
Top with capers, red onion slices, and a small handful of rocket.
Squeeze lemon juice over the top and finish with freshly ground black pepper.
Serve immediately as a luxurious brunch or light lunch.
Note: Only use sashimi-grade fresh fish when serving raw. Alternatively, use smoked or cured salmon.

prod-006.name: Teriyaki Salmon from Frozen
ingredients: 2 frozen salmon fillet portions (approx. 150 g each)
1 tbsp sake or dry white wine
1 tsp cornflour mixed with 1 tbsp water
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (180 °C fan / gas 6). Line a baking tray with parchment.
Place the frozen salmon fillets on the tray. Bake for 15 minutes to partially cook from frozen.

Meanwhile, make the teriyaki sauce: combine soy sauce, mirin, sake, and brown sugar in a small saucepan. Bring to a simmer, then stir in the cornflour mixture. Cook for 1–2 minutes until glossy and thickened.
Remove the salmon from the oven. Brush generously with the teriyaki sauce.
Return to the oven for 10–12 minutes, brushing with more sauce halfway through, until caramelised and cooked through.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and spring onion.
Serve with steamed rice and stir-fried pak choi.

prod-006.name: Salmon and Sweet Potato Tray Bake
ingredients: 2 frozen salmon fillet portions (approx. 150 g each)
1 large sweet potato, peeled and cut into 2 cm cubes
1 red pepper, deseeded and cut into chunks
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
Handful of fresh coriander, to serve
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (180 °C fan / gas 6).
Toss the sweet potato, red pepper, and red onion with olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper on a large baking tray.
Roast the vegetables for 15 minutes.
Nestle the frozen salmon fillets among the vegetables. Season the salmon with salt and pepper.
Return to the oven for 22–25 minutes until the salmon is cooked through and the vegetables are tender and caramelised at the edges.
Scatter with fresh coriander and serve with lime wedges.

prod-006.name: Salmon Pasta Bake
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
steps: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (180 °C fan / gas 6).
Poach the frozen salmon in simmering water for 12 minutes. Drain, flake into large chunks, and set aside.
Cook the penne in boiling salted water according to packet instructions. Add the frozen peas in the last 2 minutes. Drain.
In a large saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Add flour and stir for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in milk until smooth. Cook for 3–4 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Remove from heat. Stir in half the cheddar, the cream cheese, and mustard. Season with salt and pepper.
Fold in the cooked pasta, peas, and flaked salmon. Pour into a large ovenproof dish.
Top with remaining cheddar and panko breadcrumbs.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until golden and bubbling. Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
